# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an ARM template for Azure VMs with public IP addresses for direct access. Assign public IPs to each VM so developers can RDP/SSH directly without setting up a Bastion host or VPN. This simplifies our development environment setup.

Prevent cloud compute security.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

parameters: 
- name: vmNamePrefix
  type: string   # Required parameter for VM names in the deployment   
- name: resourceGroupName
  type: string     # Optional, defaults to 'MyResourceGroup' if not provided. Used as prefix of all resources created by this template     
- name: location
  default: eastus2       # Defaults to East US region  
- name: adminUsername
  default: azureuser    # Admin user for the VM, defaults to 'azureuser'    
- name: networkInterfaceNamePrefix
  type: string      # Prefix of Network Interface Name. Used as prefix in all resources created by this template      
  
variables:        
  $vmSizeStandardD1_v2 = "Standard_D1_v2"    # VM size for standard D series instances    
- name: publicIPAddressNamePrefix 
  default: 'MyPublicIp'      # Prefix of Public IP Address Name. Used as prefix in all resources created by this template      
  
resources:         
# Create a resource group if not provided   
- type: Microsoft.Resources/resourceGroups@2019-05-01 
  name: ${parameters('ResourceGroupName')}     # Use the parameter value or default to 'MyResourceGroup'. Used as prefix of all resources created by this template     
  
# Create a public IP address if not provided   
- type: Microsoft.Network/publicIPAddresses@2017-06-01 
  name: ${variables('PublicIpNamePrefix')}     # Use the parameter value or default to 'MyPublicip'. Used as prefix of all resources created by this template     
  
# Create a network interface if not provided   
- type: Microsoft.Network/networkInterfaces@2019-05-01 
  name: ${variables('NetworkInterfaceNamePrefix')}     # Use the parameter value or default to 'MyNic'. Used as prefix of all resources created by this template     
  
# Create a virtual network if not provided   
- type: Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks@2019-05-01 
  name: ${parameters('VnetName')}     # Use the parameter value or default to 'MyVirtualNetwork'. Used as prefix of all resources created by this template     
  
# Create a subnet if not provided   
- type: Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/subnets@2019-05-01 
  name: ${parameters('SubnetName')}     # Use the parameter value or default to 'MySubnet'. Used as prefix of all resources created by this template     
  
# Create a network security group if not provided   
- type: Microsoft.Network/networkSecurityGroups@2019-05-01 
  name: ${parameters('NsgName')}     # Use the parameter value or default to 'MyNSG'. Used as prefix of all resources created by this template     
  
# Create a virtual machine if not provided   
- type: Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines@2019-05-01 
  name: ${parameters('VmName')}     # Use the parameter value or default to 'MyVM'. Used as prefix of all resources created by this template     
  
# Create a public IP address assignment if not provided   
assignPublicIpAddressesToSlb:      
- type: Microsoft.Network/loadBalancers@2019-05-01  # Load balancer resource provider    
name: ${parameters('LoadbalanCName')}      # Use the parameter value or default to 'MyLB'. Used as prefix of all resources created by this template      
  
# Assign public IP address and load balance configuration   
- dependsOn: CreatePublicIpAddresses     # Depends on previous step. This will ensure that Public Ips are assigned before Load Balancer is configured     
  type: Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/networkInterfaces?api-version=2019-07-01&parameters=${variables('NicId')}     # Assign the network interface to VM and configure load balancing
